signInInformation.title: Changes to Login Process

description.text:
We have merged the login process for EuroLeagueTV and EuroLeague ID – now a single account lets you access both, plus our Fantasy Challenge game.

**If you previously just had a EuroLeagueTV account, please use the forgot password function to activate your new Euroleague ID account.**

If you previously just had a Euroleague ID account, congratulations! You now also have access to a free tier of videos on EuroLeagueTV.\*

**If you are accessing our Fantasy Challenge via our partner's website, please be informed that our partners do not process, collect nor store any user data of participating users as stated in our Privacy Policy**

signInInformation.asteriskText: *EuroLeagueTV is available worldwide excluding Spain, France, Italy and Monaco.

accountDeletionInfo.text:
Before continuing to delete your EuroLeague ID account, please bear in mind the following:

If you play the Euroleague Fantasy Challenge, you may want to delete your team there first, as it will be more difficult to do that if your account no longer exists. You can do that at [euroleaguefantasy.euroleague.net/en/account](http://euroleaguefantasy.euroleague.net/en/account).

The purpose of the data collection is to manage your application and participation in the 2024 Euroleague Basketball FanXP according to the terms and conditions and the privacy policy. The legal basis for the processing is your consent. Only those third parties to whom Euroleague Properties SA is legally obliged and the companies entrusted with internal management services will have access to the data. You can exercise, at any time, under the terms established by law, your rights of access, rectification or deletion of data, as well as request restrictions on the processing of your personal data, oppose its processing or request the portability of your data, by sending a postal communication to the address indicated above or by writing an e-mail to data.protection.officer@euroleague.net. club.info: It's been a fast rise to the top for Paris Basketball. Founded in 2018 by David Kahn, the club started with a single goal: bringing elite basketball to the City of Light. It started in the French second division when Paris bought the license of HTV Basket. The club was promoted to the French top flight at the end of the 2020-21 season. After avoiding relegation, Paris reached the 2022 Eurocup Quarterfinals in its debut season in the competition. Paris put together a record-breaking 2023-24 EuroCup campaign. Led by T.J. Shorts and Nadir Hifi, Paris won the EuroCup with a 22-1 record, winning its last 15 games to lift the trophy after a sweep of JL Bourg-en-Bresse, with Shorts chosen as the EuroCup season MVP and EuroCup Finals MVP. Paris also lifted the French Cup that season. Last season, Paris made its EuroLeague debut with a bang. The team won 10 straight games to jump atop the standings in Round 12. Paris eventually reached the EuroLeague Playoffs by shocking Real Madrid on the road in the Play-In Showdown, only to get swept in the playoffs by the eventual champion Fenerbahce Beko Istanbul. But the club also made history domestically, winning its premier French League championship. After putting Paris back on the European basketball map, the club intends to continue its upward path.

heroMedia.title: Paris overcomes ASVEL thanks to Justin Robinson’s late heroics
heroMedia.summary: A massive fourth quarter surge from Robinson powers Paris to a comeback win after a shaky first half

linkedIds.text: Paris Basketball v LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne (2026-03-12T20:00:00.000Z)

content.text:
“I think it’s a very valuable win, for many reasons,” said Francesco Tabellini, moments after his Paris Basketball team earned a 90-81 win over fellow French side LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne. “I’m super proud of my players and the way they picked it up.”  
The Italian head coach had plenty to be proud of in the second half, but it wasn’t a straightforward victory for the Parisians.

Last-placed ASVEL set the tone in the opening quarter, executing well to finish the opening period with a 6-point advantage. The visiting side kept its foot on the gas in the second, moving the ball slickly and controlling the glass to take a 9-point lead into the halftime break.

The hosts came back swinging in the second half, rallying back with a 7-0 run early in the third period to carve a route back into the contest. Though ASVEL offered some resistance, Paris poured in the points, eventually putting together a 29-point quarter and taking control of the game.

Pierric Poupet’s team fought back down the stretch, but the home team couldn’t be stopped. Paris guard Justin Robinson exploded for 12 points in the final 2 minutes of the game to propel his team over the line. When the final buzzer sounded, they had secured a 9-point victory.

The 28-year-old Robinson was the undeniable star of the show, adding 6 assists and 6 boards to a game-changing 25-point scoring performance. His back-to-back clutch threes in the fourth made a huge difference, but Robinson attributed the plays to the confidence given to him by his teammates.

“They were daggers,” said Robinson. “You can hear my teammates in the background, they give me confidence all the time. I started off slow, missed a lot of threes, but made them when it mattered. My coaches and teammates trusted me to step up big. I stepped up to the challenge and made big shots down the stretch.”

Having impressed during his first season in the French capital, Robinson seems to be enjoying every moment.

“This is who I am as a character and as a player,” Robinson shared. “I like to have fun. In those big moments down the stretch, I just try to get the crowd hyped, my teammates pumped up, and just keep going. It’s all fun and games, I’m blessed to play this game, in this beautiful league.”

Of course, Robinson couldn’t win the game alone. Allan Dokossi was a highly effective supporting act, recording 12 points and 5 rebounds and Lamar Stevens added 11 points. Overall, Tabellini was encouraged by the way his team performed in the end.

“We started the game pretty poorly, shooting-wise, and lost a bit of our trust. Then we kept our composure and didn’t allow them to take a big lead, so in the second half it was completely different, we put together great offense, it was amazing.”

View this post on Instagram

A post shared by Paris Basketball 🏀 (@parisbasketball)

On the other bench, Poupet was understandably disappointed with the loss, but he sees it as yet another learning opportunity.

“We had a great first half, then a good 5 minutes at the end, but it wasn’t enough,” Poupet said. “We had a long stretch without scoring or making stops. We have to learn from it, we cannot play 25 good minutes, we have to make it 30, 35 or 40 maybe, so that we can win games.”

Poupet may hope for significant improvements, but ASVEL will now play for a combination of pride and future development. The loss moves his team to 8-23 for the season and mathematically eliminates it from playoff qualification.

Paris’s record improves to 12-19, as it holds onto an outside chance of postseason action. Tabellini’s men will face their next challenge on Tuesday, when they meet Hapoel IBI Tel Aviv.

linkedId.text: Paris Basketball v LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne (2026-03-12T20:00:00.000Z)

articleMetadata.title: Paris overcomes ASVEL thanks to Justin Robinson’s late heroics
articleMetadata.description: Paris Basketball earned a 90–81 win over LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne behind a spectacular late surge from Justin Robinson, who tallied 25 points, 6 rebounds and 6 assists, including 12 points in the final two minutes. ASVEL controlled the first half, but Paris stormed back with a 29 point third quarter and strong supporting contributions from Allan Dokossi and Lamar Stevens. The victory improves Paris to 12–19 and preserves its slim postseason chances, while ASVEL falls to 8–23 and is eliminated from playoff contention.

heroMedia.title: Olympiacos shoots the lights out to celebrate 101st club birthday with road win at Paris
heroMedia.summary: The Reds set a new club record for triples made in a half by going 13-for-19 from deep before the break in the French capital

content.text:
Riding its momentum after a hard-fought home victory in the Greek derby against Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens last week, [Olympiacos Piraeus](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/teams/olympiacos-piraeus/oly/) fired on all cylinders from beyond the arc to crush [Paris Basketball](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/paris-basketball/prs/) 87-104 on Tuesday night.

The Reds celebrated their 101st birthday with a dominant outing in the French capital, demonstrating their prowess on both ends of the floor at adidas arena.

"It's a special day for Olympiacos," head coach Georgios Bartzokas said in the postgame press conference. "Today it's our birthday, 101 years of history, greatness and titles. We're feeling privileged that we're representing Olympiacos at this time and we are happy about our fans who came here from all over Europe to support us."

content.title: Paris Basketball-Olympiacos Piraeus | Round 34 Highlights | 2025-26 EuroLeague

content.text:
Olympiacos hit the ground running, with [Tyler Dorsey](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/tyler-dorsey/009849/) going 3-for-4 and [Alec Peters](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/alec-peters/008829/) a perfect 4-for-4 from deep for a 22-33 lead after 10 minutes. The guests tied the club's all-time record for most three-pointers made in a first quarter by going 8-for-10 from downtown.

"No matter if it's me or someone else, we've got to start games off with that fire," Dorsey told EuroLeague TV after the game. "That leads into the momentum into the first quarter and even to the rest of the game. So, it's important for me starting off games with that fire and being ready from the jump ball to go full speed."

Playing its usual high-pace basketball, Paris bounced back in the second quarter, outscoring Olympiacos 33-30 and preventing the visitors from pulling away despite the Reds setting yet another club record. In fact, Olympiacos ended the first half with 13 made triples – its most in a single half since EuroLeague data has been collected.

"Paris is an amazing team offensively with the pace that they play," Bartzokas told EuroLeague TV. "Imagine that we had 13 three-pointers made in the first half and we couldn't lead by a huge difference because they have this amazing potential and talent to score immediately in the first 5-6 seconds."

It took some tactical adjustments at halftime for Olympiacos to flip the switch defensively after the break, holding Paris to 16 points in each of the last two quarters.

"We talked it out at halftime, made some great adjustments," Peters said. "Their guards were having too much rhythm, they were too comfortable in the first half. So, for us to come out in the second half when they had 55 in the first half and they end up with 87, that's Olympiacos, that's the defense we want to bring."

What a party! 🔴⚪️

Thank you from the bottom of our hearts! 🙏🏽

Once again, you were amazing! 🙌🏽#OlympiacosBC #WeAreOlympiacos #TogetherWeFight #PBBOLY pic.twitter.com/BjsiHqUUYk

— Olympiacos B.C. (@Olympiacos_BC) March 10, 2026

The Reds came up with some key stops on defense and went on a 0-13 surge in the closing stages of the third quarter to take a 71-87 lead into the fourth. Those 87 points scored saw Olympiacos set a second-best club record for most points netted after 30 minutes.

"It's basketball and winning on the road is hard, especially in EuroLeague," Peters added. "If you can pull out a run like that, 13-0, you are going to go into the fourth feeling like you have the game. We kept it there, we controlled it and we were able to finish it."

Dorsey and Peters drilled four triples each, with [Evan Fournier](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/evan-fournier/013380/) netting three of his own. Seven Olympiacos players managed to score from beyond the arc, with the Reds going a sublime 17-for-30 (56.7%) from three-point range.

Having secured its fourth win in five games, Olympiacos is set to travel south to take on AS Monaco on Thursday. And should it manage to combine the first half's offensive display with the second half's defensive outing, the Reds could end their irregular double-round week in style.

articleMetadata.title: Olympiacos shoots the lights out to celebrate 101st club birthday with road win at Paris
articleMetadata.description: Riding its momentum after a hard-fought home victory in the Greek derby against Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens last week, Olympiacos Piraeus fired on all cylinders from beyond the arc to crush Paris Basketball 87-104 on Tuesday night.

heroMedia.title: Stats review: Turning up the pressure
heroMedia.summary: Sportradar's Matt Kamalsky dives into the defensive numbers

content.text:
With eight games remaining in the EuroLeague regular season, the early separation found by the teams in the playoff picture has nearly disappeared as the team in ninth place now sits only one game clear of the team in twelfth. While the competitiveness of the league has evolved over time, so too have some of the strategies teams are using to find an edge.

One of the less glaring examples of that is the application of full-court pressure. During the 2015-16 season, the average team sent defenders to guard the ball in the backcourt only 5.1% of the time with many teams only pressing to foul and only three teams pressing on more than 10% of the possessions they faced. This season, 10% is the EuroLeague average with eight teams eclipsing that mark and two teams pressing on over 22% of the possessions they face.

Some of that can be traced to intent. Many of the teams pressing the most a decade ago were hunting deflections to generate turnovers. There was a reluctance for many years to apply pressure beyond half court as a result – it felt risky to give the caliber of guards at this level a running start if things went wrong. However, coaches have found a comfort level using pressure more passively to disrupt tempo, try to get the ball out of certain players' hands in early offense, or simply leverage their depth instead of aggressively trying to force mistakes.

The table above lists the teams picking up full court most frequently this season. For reference, the league average for turnover percentage sits at just over 14% for all possessions. This group represents teams from across the standings, all finding varying degrees of success and implementing their pressure in different ways.

[Paris Basketball](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/teams/paris-basketball/prs/) and [Valencia Basket](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/teams/valencia-basket/pam/) are the two teams picking up the ball over the midcourt line most frequently this season. While Paris has posted similar defensive efficiency numbers whether pressing or not, Valencia has been harder to score on when pressing, often burning time off the shot clock and only having their press broken for transition opportunities 5% of the time – a tiny, impressive number at any level of basketball. Valencia does a stellar job eliminating those outcomes, largely mitigating the risk that made coaches reluctant to apply pressure a decade ago.

While EuroLeague teams typically don't rely on the wide variety of press schemes often seen at lower levels, an occasional trapping zone still pops up here and there. Even without as many drastic variations, coaches create variety in other ways. Some teams deploy specific defenders to hound the ball without committing a second defender, others focus on disrupting the inbound pass, and some, like [Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/teams/crvena-zvezda-meridianbet-belgrade/red/), adjust their pressing frequency from game to game based on their scouting report.

[Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/panathinaikos-aktor-athens/pan/) is perhaps the best example of a team trapping to force mistakes this season, as they have come up with quite a few deflections springing timely traps off the inbound pass or just over half court. Generating a turnover on around a quarter of the possessions they press is an impressive result that makes a couple of extra run-outs here or there a minor footnote.

As the season begins to wind down, the little things teams are doing continue to add up and figure to play a role in who gets into the field and who keeps home-court advantage.

articleMetadata.title: Stats review: Turning up the pressure
articleMetadata.description: With eight games remaining in the EuroLeague regular season, the early separation found by the teams in the playoff picture has nearly disappeared as the team in ninth place now sits only one game clear of the team in twelfth. While the competitiveness of the league has evolved over time, so too have some of the strategies teams are using to find an edge.

heroMedia.title: Paris pulls away in second quarter against Baskonia to claim third straight road win
heroMedia.summary: Nadir Hifi and Justin Robinson combined for 40 points

linkedIds.text: Kosner Baskonia Vitoria-Gasteiz v Paris Basketball (2026-03-06T19:30:00.000Z)

content.text:
[Paris Basketball](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/teams/paris-basketball/prs/) surged to an 81-97 road win over [Kosner Baskonia Vitoria-Gasteiz](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/baskonia-vitoria-gasteiz/bas/), extending its winning streak away from home to three consecutive victories and improving to 11-18. A second-quarter burst sparked by quick drives and sharp shooting put the visitors firmly in control, and Paris never allowed the hosts back into the game.

Coming off two crucial road wins over Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens and FC Barcelona, Paris carried its momentum into Friday's contest from the opening quarter. The visitors set the pace early, scoring easy baskets and building a 25-point second-quarter lead that put them firmly in control heading into halftime.

Paris thrived on its pace, with [Nadir Hifi](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/nadir-hifi/012640/) setting the tone and keeping Baskonia's defense on its heels. The sharpshooter poured in 21 points, standing out as the driving force behind the victory.

"We played well, we played together," Hifi said after the game. "We moved the ball and obviously played good defense too. We controlled the rebounds. A great game, and we're proud tonight."

content.title: Kosner Baskonia Vitoria-Gasteiz-Paris Basketball | Round 30 Highlights | 2025-26 EuroLeague

content.text:
Another key factor in Paris's dominance was its outside shooting. The visitors connected on 43.6% of their three-pointers, with eight different players hitting from beyond the arc. Rebounding also fueled the win, as Paris converted 21 points off second-chance opportunities.

[Justin Robinson](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/justin-robinson/014008/) was another steady force for Paris, finishing with 19 points including four three-pointers, while [Jared Rhoden](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/jared-rhoden/014178/) added 16 points and matched him from deep. [Allan Dokossi](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/allan-dokossi/014010/) stood out on both ends of the floor despite scoring just 2 points, contributing 11 rebounds and 6 assists.

"I think we got a little scared at the end of the game, but from the beginning we imposed our tempo and kept playing together, passing the ball, rebounding well," said the French center after the game. "I think that's why we got the win."

In the fourth quarter, Baskonia attempted a comeback, but Paris's defense kept the hosts from closing the gap. Paris coach Francesco Tabellini was pleased with the team's defensive effort.

"In the first half we made many threes, but I think we built those shots well, and the confidence came from the defense," Tabellini said after the game. "I think we really pushed Baskonia out of their comfort zone and took away some of the certainties they have. Defensively we were really solid all evening, except for a brief lapse in the fourth quarter, but overall the guys did a very good job."

La troisième victoire de suite en EuroLeague ! 🔥🔥🔥
On se retrouve ENFIN à la maison ce mardi face à l’Olympiakos !

La billetterie est ici : https://t.co/QuNVs8ZJIK pic.twitter.com/oelpoQ848L

— Paris Basketball (@ParisBasketball) March 6, 2026

For Baskonia, [Eugene Omoruyi](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/eugene-omoruyi/014207/) led the way with 17 points, while [Clement Frisch](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/clement-frisch/014026/) stood out in the third quarter with 13 points and 6 rebounds. [Timothe Luwawu-Cabarrot](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/timothe-luwawu-cabarrot/012080/) also stepped up in moments when the offense stalled, adding 13 points, but it wasn't enough.

"It's a jumble till the end," Paolo Galbiatti said after the game. "I can accept losing – they know that – if the others are better. Tonight they played a better game than us, but we weren't competitive by EuroLeague standards, and that's something we need to adjust."

linkedId.text: Kosner Baskonia Vitoria-Gasteiz v Paris Basketball (2026-03-06T19:30:00.000Z)

articleMetadata.title: Paris pulls away in second quarter against Baskonia to claim third straight road win
articleMetadata.description: Paris Basketball surged to an 81-97 road win over Kosner Baskonia Vitoria-Gasteiz, extending its winning streak away from home to three consecutive victories and improving to 11-18. A second-quarter burst sparked by quick drives and sharp shooting put the visitors firmly in control, and Paris never allowed the hosts back into the game.

The EuroLeague Regular Season Round 21 game between Hapoel IBI Tel Aviv and Paris Basket that was scheduled to be played on Tuesday, March 3 in Tel Aviv has been suspended due to the current situation in the region.

Euroleague Basketball will evaluate with the affected teams the best possible options to reschedule the game. Further information will be released in the coming days.

heroMedia.title: Pulsating Paris poop Panathinaikos’s party as Hayes-Davis debut falls short
heroMedia.summary: The fast-improving French team grabbed a second straight eye-catching road win

linkedIds.text: Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens v Paris Basketball (2026-02-26T19:45:00.000Z)

content.text:
The last time we saw [Nigel Hayes-Davis](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/nigel-hayes-davis/008962/) on a EuroLeague court, he was scoring 23 points in last season’s title decider to help Fenerbahce Beko Istanbul claim the championship crown and earn himself the Final Four MVP award.

Fast forward nine months and one day, and Hayes-Davis was back on the Old Continent, now tasked with relaunching the title quest of a power-packed but inconsistent [Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/panathinaikos-aktor-athens/pan/).

On first glance, that will be a considerable challenge. Hayes-Davis’s debut was ruined by a fully merited road win for [Paris Basketball](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/paris-basketball/prs/), which led for the last 27 minutes and flirted with a club record in three-point shooting on the way to a 99-104 victory, which was mostly much more comfortable than the final scoreline suggested.

Panathinaikos has now lost seven of its last 11 EuroLeague games, including several setbacks against teams in the bottom half of the standings as Paris followed the recent examples set by Partizan Mozzart Bet Belgrade, Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv, FC Bayern Munich and EA7 Emporio Armani Milan. If the negative run continues for much longer, Panathinaikos will find itself in that lower half.

content.title: Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens-Paris Basketball | Round 29 Highlights | 2025-26 EuroLeague

content.text:
Offensive production during the recent injury absence of [Kendrick Nunn](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/kendrick-nunn/012774/) – who also missed this game through illness – was a recurring issue in many of those losses, but on this occasion, the problem was at the other end of the court as Paris roared to its joint second-highest scoring night on the road in the club’s two-season EuroLeague history.

The French team played with its customary terrific tempo right from the opening tip, initially taking control with a 2-10 first-quarter run, scoring 3 of its first 4 three-point attempts and reaching 6-of-10 from deep by the end of the first quarter. And there was apparently nothing that the Greens could do, with the pattern continuing as Paris maintained its run-and-gun, shoot-on-sight policy – when the halftime buzzer arrived, the visitors had splashed 10-of-22 from beyond the arc to race into a 45-58 interval lead.

That scoring pace couldn’t be maintained, but Paris always had enough offense – from a variety of sources – to comfortably hold off the host’s half-hearted resistance until none other than Hayes-Davis delivered a remarkable blitz of 11 points in 2 minutes to spark an improbable late comeback. The debutant even had a chance to win the game, but his three-point attempt inside the final 10 seconds was off the mark – leading to the ejection of home coach Ergin Ataman as he protested a no-call on the shot.

View this post on Instagram

A post shared by EuroLeague (@euroleague)

In truth, though, Paris was the better team for the vast majority of the game. As much as the postgame analysis will focus on the Greens’ ongoing shortcomings, the French team deserves a huge amount of credit for a second consecutive eyebrow-raising road win following a 74-85 success at FC Barcelona before the mini-break. And with the last month also containing a home win over Real Madrid and a 2-point loss to Fenerbahce, it’s clear that Francesco Tabellini’s men are heading quickly in the right direction.

“We are doing good, I’m very happy for my players,” Tabellini enthused. “They deserve to get some reward from all the effort they are putting in practices. Tonight they gave a great effort, and despite some tough mistakes at the end of the game, we really deserved the win. It was a nice one.

“It was important to stick to our identity. All the time we were aggressive, we created problems, and when we sped up the tempo of the game, we were efficient. It’s up to us to dictate and impose the type of game that we want to play.”

View this post on Instagram

A post shared by EuroLeague (@euroleague)

Once again, Paris’s offense was led by one of the prime contenders for this season’s Alphonso Ford Top Scorer Trophy, [Nadir Hifi](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/nadir-hifi/012640/). The 23-year-old Prince of Paris drained 20 points on 4-of-7 three-point shooting, keeping himself at the top of the EuroLeague’s scoring charts.

And, if anything, he sounded a little disappointed that his team’s recent form took so long to arrive.

“We stayed together for 40 minutes, we played our game and were aggressive,” Hifi said. “Now we can look forward to our next games – we keep working and improving. We could [have done] better if we had played like this from the beginning [of the season] and we had been consistent during the year, but we’ve got to live with this.”

In the next few weeks, living with Paris will be quite a challenge for its opponents.

articleMetadata.title: Pulsating Paris poop Panathinaikos’s party as Hayes-Davis debut falls short
articleMetadata.description: Paris Basketball earned a 99–104 road win over Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens, leading for the final 27 minutes and flirting with a club mark from deep. Nadir Hifi (20 points, 4‑of‑7 3FG) paced an attack that hit 10 triples by halftime and maintained control despite Nigel Hayes‑Davis’s late 11‑point blitz and a frantic finish that saw Ergin Ataman ejected. The victory follows Paris’s 74–85 triumph at Barcelona and a recent home win over Real Madrid, underscoring the French team’s rapid rise.

heroMedia.title: Phase 2 general public tickets for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad sell out

content.text:
The extraordinary demand for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens Presented by Etihad shows no signs of slowing down, as all tickets made available during Phase 2 of public sales have officially sold out.

When Phase 2 opened on February 26 at 10:00 CET, fans once again responded in massive numbers. Following Phase 1, which saw more than one million fans connect to the ticketing platform and resulted in a complete sellout, the second allocation was also quickly exhausted, reaffirming the immense appeal of European basketball’s premier event.

The overwhelming global interest highlights the unique atmosphere and significance of the Final Four, with fans from across Europe and beyond eager to secure their place in Athens for the season’s ultimate showdown.

Fans who were unable to purchase tickets during General public tickets sale Phase 2 will still have an additional opportunity on February 26 as VIP tickets will be available starting at 14:00 CET.

Fans who were unable to secure tickets during Phase 1 will still have multiple opportunities to attend European basketball’s premier event. The second public ticket sales phase will open on February 26 at 10:00 CET. The third public ticket sales phase will open on March 17 at 10:00 CET, while the final sales phase will begin on April 20 at 10:00 CEST, coinciding with the conclusion of the Regular Season as teams continue their pursuit of a Final Four berth.

Each remaining phase will feature a limited allocation across multiple price categories, and high demand is expected once again.

All ticketing details for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens Presented by Etihad are available at [https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/](https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/).

heroMedia.title: Power rankings: After the cups have gone
heroMedia.summary: Our experts offer up a suggestion for every team through 28 rounds

content.text:
Welcome to the latest edition of the EuroLeague Power Rankings. The list is the brainchild of [EuroLeague.net](http://EuroLeague.net)'s team of experts, which is made up of Geoff Gillingham, Javier Gancedo, Frankie Sachs and Igor Petrinovic. The rankings are not a prediction of what the final standings will look like, but rather where all 20 teams stand – in their humble opinions – today, after Round 28.

 

### 1. Fenerbahce Beko Istanbul (20-7)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Besiktas on Sunday to win the Turkish Cup

**The question we are asking:** Is Fenerbahce aware that not team to finish the regular season in first place under the current format has won the championship?

 

### 2. Olympiacos Piraeus (18-9)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Panathinaikos in the Greek Cup final on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Do we need to start worrying about the health of the Reds’ stars?

 

### 3. Real Madrid (17-11)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Baskonia in the Spanish Cup final on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** Can *Los Blancos* improve its defense to become a top contender?

### 4. Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Olympiacos to win the Greek Cup on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Will the addition of Nigel Hayes-Davis be enough to put the Greens over the top?

 

### 5. Valencia Basket (18-10)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Real Madrid in the Spanish Cup semifinal on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** How will Valencia shake its disappointment from not winning the Spanish Cup at home? 

 

### 6. FC Barcelona (17-11)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Barcelona in the Spanish Cup semifinal on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** With Punter back in action, can Barça improve its consistency and stay in the top-six race?

 

### 7. Zalgiris Kaunas (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Rytas Vilnius in the Lithuanian Cup final on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** With six home games remaining and good momentum, is a top-six finish a realistic goal?

### 8. AS Monaco (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Le Mans to win the French Leaders Cup on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** After a turbulent stretch on and off the court, can this trophy galvanize Monaco and stabilize its EuroLeague push?

 

### 9. Hapoel IBI Tel Aviv (16-11)

**What they did last week:** Went 3-0 in the Israeli League

**The question we are asking:** Is Hapoel’s recent slide just a bump in the road or a sign that the team peaked too early?

 

### 10. Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Mega Belgrade to win the Serbian Cup on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Is Jordan Nwora the star who can carry Zvezda to new heights? 

 

### 11. Dubai Basketball (14-14)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Bosna Sarajevo in the ABA League

**The question we are asking:** Now that Dzanan Musa is back and in shape, can Dubai win enough games to make it to the Play-In Showdown?

### 12. Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv (13-15)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Bnei Herzliya to win the Israeli Cup on Thursday

**The question we are asking:** Is there enough time left in the season for Maccabi to climb to 10th place?

 

### 13. EA7 Emporio Armani Milan (14-14)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Tortona to win the Italian Cup on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** Can Milan translate domestic momentum into EuroLeague consistency and revive its play-in hopes?

 

### 14. FC Bayern Munich (12-16)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Bamberg in German Cup semifinals on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** What would this team have looked like with a full season under Coach Pesic?

 

### 15. Anadolu Efes Istanbul (9-19)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Besiktas in the Turkish Cup semifinal on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Is Efes destined to become a spoiler for a few teams in the playoff race?

### 16. Virtus Bologna (12-16)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Tortona in the Italian Cup semifinals on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** What adjustments can Dusko Ivanovic make to halt Virtus’s slide – especially away from home?

 

### 17. Partizan Mozzart Bet Belgrade (9-19)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Mega Belgrade in the Serbian Cup semifinals on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Can Joan Penarroya realign the locker room and restore Partizan’s competitive edge?

 

### 18. Paris Basketball (9-18)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Bourg in the French Leader’s Cup quarterfinals on Friday

**The question we are asking:** What will it take for Paris to recapture its early-season identity and get Nadir Hifi back to his best?

 

### 19. Kosner Baskonia Vitoria-Gasteiz (9-19)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Real Madrid in the Spanish Cup final on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** After its Spanish Cup success and strong home performances, can Baskonia start winning consistently on the road?

 

### 20. LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne (7-21)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Monaco in the French Cup quarterfinals on Tuesday

**The question we are asking:** With the postseason out of reach, which players are auditioning for their EuroLeague futures?

heroMedia.title: 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad – Ticket sales Phase 2 to start on February 26
heroMedia.summary: Phase 1 general public ticket sale attracted over 1 million fans worldwide

content.text:
Ticket sales for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad will enter their next chapter on February 26 at 10:00 CET, as Phase 2 opens to the general public.

The launch of Phase 2 coincides with the closing of the EuroLeague players’ market, ensuring that fans will have a clear picture of each team’s final roster.

All fans,  except those who have already purchased tickets during previous sales phases or pre-sales , will be eligible to access the February 26 general public sale starting at 10:00 CET. During this second sales window,  all fans will also have the opportunity to purchase VIP tickets starting at 14:00 CET .

Ticketing information is available at [https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/](https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/). Fans must log in or create a EuroLeague ID account to complete their purchase. Once logged in, all available categories will be displayed. Due to expected high demand and to ensure fairness, each fan may purchase a maximum of two tickets.

For the first time, Final Four tickets are being released in multiple sales phases. This approach is designed to expand access for the EuroLeague’s growing global fan base, offering multiple opportunities to secure tickets,  whether fans plan to attend regardless of the qualified teams or prefer to wait until the competition reaches its decisive stages. All phases follow a fair access policy, meaning that fans who purchase tickets in one phase will not be eligible to participate in subsequent phases. This measure is designed to ensure broader access to the event.

Phase 1, held on February 11, experienced massive demand, as over a million fans connected to sold out all available tickets in less than five minutes, once again demonstrating the enormous appeal of the EuroLeague’s premier event.

The 2026 EuroLeague Final Four semifinals will be played on Friday, May 22, at 18:00 and 21:00 local time (17:00 and 20:00 CEST). On Sunday, May 24, the adidas NextGen EuroLeague Championship Game will tip off at 18:00 local time (17:00 CEST), while the season will culminate with the Championship Game at 21:00 local time (20:00 CEST), when a new EuroLeague champion will be crowned.

A third ticket sales window will open on Tuesday, March 17, at 10:00 CET, offering another opportunity for fans to secure their seats. The final sales phase will launch on Monday, April 20, at 10:00 CEST, positioned between the conclusion of the Regular Season and the start of the Play-In Showdown, with 10 teams still in contention for a place in the Final Four.

All ticketing details for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad are available at [https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/](https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/).
